name: Wild Mushroom Risotto
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Dry white wine or broth
recipeInstructions:
You can substitute dried wild mushrooms for the shiitake. Just soak them for 15-to-20 minutes beforehand (and be sure to add the strained soaking liquid to the stock). Heat butter in a small skillet over moderate heat. When it begins to foam, add mushrooms and cook until soft. Add salt and pepper to taste. Turn off the heat and set aside.
Bring the broth to a steady simmer in a saucepan on top of the stove. In a heavy 4-quart casserole, heat butter and oil over moderate heat. Add onion and sauté for 1-to-2 minutes until it begins to soften, being careful not to brown. Add rice to the butter-oil-onion mixture. Using a wooden spoon, stir for 1 minute, making sure all grains are well coated.
Add wine and stir until completely absorbed. Gradually add simmering broth, about half a cup at a time, stirring frequently. Wait until each addition is almost completely absorbed (approximately 2 minutes) before adding the next half cup, reserving one quarter cup to add at the end. Stir often.
After approximately 18 minutes (rice should be tender and firm), stir in the reserved broth, mushrooms, Mascarpone cheese, and Parmesan cheese. Continue stirring vigorously so cheeses melt. Sprinkle with parsley. Serve immediately.
